commands.review_summary:
The branch factors agent runtime config selection into a shared helper, applies local-model Tool Search defaults after selecting the resolved runtime snapshot, and adds a Tavily-shaped SecretRef regression test.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +81. Total +103 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main applies Tool Search defaults to the authored config before plugin runtime config selection, so an overlay clone can bypass the active resolved SecretRef snapshot.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainer review should accept or reject the auth-provider-sensitive runtime snapshot boundary and wait for required checks; no narrow automated repair is currently identified.

target.title: fix: keep Tavily exec SecretRefs resolved in Tool Search
target.body:
Related: #95109

## Summary

- Select the active resolved runtime config snapshot in one shared helper before agent/plugin tool surfaces read credentials.
- Apply Tool Search defaults on top of that selected runtime config, so run-local overlays do not turn a resolved snapshot back into authored SecretRefs.
- Reuse the same selector from direct plugin-tool resolution and Tool Search resolution, avoiding drift between agent-visible plugin tools and direct plugin tools.
- Add a regression test with a Tavily-shaped exec SecretRef proving the agent tool surface receives the in-memory resolved value while the source config remains a SecretRef.

## Root Cause

The failing layer was the agent Tool Search runtime-config overlay, not Tavily itself.

Before this change, the embedded attempt path and harness bridge called `applyLocalModelLeanToolSearchDefaults` directly on `params.config`. When `params.config` was the authored source config, that helper cloned the config to add `tools.toolSearch`. The clone no longer matched the active runtime source snapshot, so later plugin-tool config selection treated it as an explicit config and did not swap in the already-resolved runtime snapshot.

Tavily then received `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey` as the original exec-backed SecretRef. Its strict runtime helper correctly reported the unresolved SecretRef instead of inventing a fallback. The runtime secret resolver had already done the right work; the agent tool layer was bypassing that resolved in-memory snapshot.

RCA proof:

- Before behavior in source: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` and `src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts` applied local Tool Search defaults to `params.config` before runtime snapshot selection.
- Existing selector proof: `src/agents/openclaw-plugin-tools.ts` already had the same source/runtime snapshot matching rule for direct plugin tools, so this PR moves that rule into `src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts` and reuses it.
- Runtime/plugin contract inspected: plugin tools receive `context.config` / `context.runtimeConfig` from the agent tool surface, and Tavily expects this runtime config to contain resolved secret values before `resolveTavilyApiKey` reads it.
- The regression only appears when an agent-visible Tool Search overlay is added, because the overlay clone stops matching the active runtime source snapshot.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with Tavily configured through an exec-backed SecretRef could pass config and secret audit, and the key could work directly against Tavily, but agent-visible Tavily tools still failed with an unresolved SecretRef when Tool Search materialized plugin tools.

This is related to #95109 because the visible error is Tavily SecretRef resolution, but this PR does not relax Tavily's SecretRef fallback behavior. It fixes the core agent Tool Search runtime-config layer so exec/file-backed resolved runtime snapshots keep flowing to plugin tools.

## Why This Change Was Made

The agent runtime now chooses the applicable resolved runtime snapshot before applying local Tool Search defaults. That keeps run-local config overlays in memory, preserves the authored SecretRef in source config, and avoids storing plaintext credentials.

The core selector is deliberately shared between direct plugin-tool construction and Tool Search construction because both paths decide whether plugin tools receive resolved SecretRef material.

## User Impact

Agent-visible Tavily tools can use an exec-backed SecretRef that has already been resolved into the active runtime snapshot. Users do not need to convert the credential to plaintext config or restart services to get the agent tool surface to see the resolved key.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior fixed: Tool Search runtime config now carries the resolved in-memory Tavily API key value to agent/plugin tools while the source config keeps the exec SecretRef.

Closest feasible environment tested: a real runtime path using `resolveRuntimeWebTools`, `setRuntimeConfigSnapshot`, `createAgentHarnessToolSurfaceRuntime`, `resolveOpenClawPluginToolsForOptions`, and the actual `tavily_search` tool. The exec provider returned a generated dummy value only, and the Tavily tool posted to a public HTTPS echo endpoint instead of Tavily so no real credential was exposed.

Manual proof steps:

1. Create a temporary exec provider script outside the repo that logs only call count and returns `protocolVersion: 1` values for `tavily/api-key`.
2. Configure Tavily with `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey` as `{ source: "exec", provider: "vault", id: "tavily/api-key" }` and `baseUrl` set to `https://httpbin.org/anything/openclaw-pr-97827`.
3. Resolve web-tool runtime secrets, activate the runtime snapshot, build the agent Tool Search runtime, materialize `tavily_search`, and execute it.
4. Delete the temporary provider script.

Copied result:

```text
PR #97827 exec SecretRef Tool Search proof
external endpoint: https://httpbin.org/anything/openclaw-pr-97827/search
credential used: generated dummy value only; no real Tavily key
exec provider calls: 1
source still SecretRef: exec:vault:tavily/api-key
resolved runtime plugin key is string: true
agent Tool Search config plugin key is string: true
agent Tool Search config has defaults: true
materialized tool: tavily_search
tool executed successfully: true
result provider: tavily
result query matched: true
unresolved SecretRef error observed: no
```

Observed after behavior:

- `resolveRuntimeWebTools` invokes the exec provider once and writes the resolved dummy value only into the in-memory runtime config.
- `createAgentHarnessToolSurfaceRuntime` returns a Tool Search runtime config whose Tavily config contains a string API key.
- The original source config still contains `{ source: "exec", provider: "vault", id: "tavily/api-key" }`.
- `resolveOpenClawPluginToolsForOptions` materializes the real `tavily_search` tool from that agent Tool Search config.
- Executing `tavily_search` returns Tavily-formatted output and does not report an unresolved SecretRef.
- No plaintext real credential is written to config, committed, or printed.

## Evidence

Focused tests:

```shell
pnpm test src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.browser-plugin.integration.test.ts
# Test Files  2 passed (2); Tests 10 passed (10)
```

Formatting, lint, whitespace:

```shell
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts src/agents/tool-search-runtime-config.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/openclaw-plugin-tools.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts src/agents/tool-search-runtime-config.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/openclaw-plugin-tools.ts
# passed with no diagnostics

git diff --check
# passed
```

Autoreview:

```shell
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
# autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
# overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

Requested update command:

```shell
oc-update
# completed once before the PR branch refresh
```

Blocked check:

```shell
pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/tool-runtime-config.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.ts src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts
# blocked before running: ../crabbox/bin/crabbox version 0.10.1-dev; Blacksmith Testbox requires Crabbox >= 0.22.0
```

## Verification

- Focused regression: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/tool-surface-bridge.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.browser-plugin.integration.test.ts`
- Real-path proof with generated dummy exec SecretRef through agent Tool Search and actual `tavily_search`
- Formatting: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`
- Lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`
- Whitespace: `git diff --check`
- Autoreview: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Update command requested by reporter: `oc-update` completed once

## What was not tested

- Live Tavily API execution with a real exec-backed SecretRef, to avoid exposing or storing real credentials.
- A loopback HTTP proof endpoint, because the repo's SSRF guard correctly blocks private/internal loopback targets; the real-path proof used a public HTTPS echo endpoint with a generated dummy value instead.
- Full `pnpm check:changed`, because the repo wrapper delegated to Blacksmith Testbox and stopped before running with the local Crabbox version mismatch noted above.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted with Codex. I understand the change: it keeps the active resolved runtime snapshot as the base config for agent Tool Search overlays and direct plugin tools, so plugin tools receive resolved in-memory SecretRef values without persisting plaintext credentials.
target.author: vacinc
